# System Context Overview ## Document Information | Attribute | Value | |-----------|-------| | Document Title | ChronicCareAIPlatform - C4 System Context Architecture Document | | Document Version | 1.0 | | Generation Time | 2025-01-09 | | Confidence Score | 92% | --- ## 1. Project Introduction ### 1.1 Project Overview **Project Name:** ChronicCareAIPlatform **Project Description:** A microservices-based chronic disease care platform designed for comprehensive healthcare management. The system enables patients to upload and manage health reports, empowers doctors to manage schedules and patient care workflows, and provides AI-powered schedule recommendations to optimize clinical operations. ### 1.2 Core Functionality and Business Value The ChronicCareAIPlatform delivers significant value to healthcare stakeholders by: | Value Proposition | Description | |-------------------|-------------| | **Streamlined Care Management** | Connects patients with doctors through digital health records, reducing administrative overhead | | **Automated Scheduling** | AI-assisted care planning and schedule recommendations optimize doctor productivity | | **Improved Patient Engagement** | Digital health report management increases patient participation in their care journey | | **Care Continuity** | Centralized health records ensure seamless information flow across care episodes | | **Multi-Channel Communication** | Integrated SMS, email, and in-app notifications keep all stakeholders informed | ### 1.3 Technical Characteristics Overview The platform is built on a modern microservices architecture with the following key technical characteristics: ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ Technical Foundation │ ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ Runtime Framework │ Spring Boot 3.x + Dubbo 3.x RPC │ │ Data Layer │ MySQL + MyBatis-Plus + Redis │ │ Security │ JWT (Dual-Token) + Spring Security │ │ Service Discovery │ Nacos │ │ Rate Limiting │ Alibaba Sentinel │ │ Cloud Storage │ Alibaba Cloud OSS │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` **Architecture Patterns:** - **Microservices Architecture**: Clear separation of concerns with domain-driven service boundaries - **Three-Tier Module Pattern**: API/Consumer/Provider layers promoting interface-driven development - **Dual-Token Authentication**: Access tokens (30 min) + Refresh tokens (7 days) with Redis-backed session management - **RBAC Authorization**: Role-based access control with expandable action-based permissions - **Sensitive Data Encryption**: AES-CBC encryption at the persistence layer via MyBatis TypeHandler --- ## 2. Target Users ### 2.1 User Role Definitions The ChronicCareAIPlatform serves four distinct user personas, each with specific access rights and functional requirements: ```mermaid mindmap root((ChronicCareAI Platform Users)) Patient Upload health reports View personal records Receive notifications Access doctor info Doctor Manage schedules View patient reports AI recommendations Complete consultations Family Member View authorized info Emergency notifications System Administrator Manage accounts Configure permissions Monitor system ``` ### 2.2 Detailed User Profiles #### 2.2.1 Patient | Attribute | Description | |-----------|-------------| | **Role Code** | `PATIENT` | | **Description** | Individuals with chronic conditions requiring ongoing medical care and health monitoring | | **Primary Permissions** | `PATIENT:READ`, `PATIENT:WRITE` | **Key Needs:** - Upload and manage health reports in multiple formats (images, PDFs, text) - View personal health records with full access to historical data - Access information about their assigned attending doctor - Receive timely notifications via SMS and email for appointments and health reminders **Usage Scenarios:** 1. Patient uploads a lab report PDF after a routine checkup 2. Patient reviews their health history before a doctor consultation 3. Patient receives SMS notification about an upcoming appointment #### 2.2.2 Doctor | Attribute | Description | |-----------|-------------| | **Role Code** | `DOCTOR` | | **Description** | Healthcare providers managing chronic disease patients | | **Primary Permissions** | `DOCTOR:READ`, `DOCTOR:WRITE`, `DOCTOR:ALL` | **Key Needs:** - View patient health reports with appropriate permission controls - Manage daily schedules and appointments efficiently - Receive AI-generated schedule recommendations for optimized workflow - Complete consultations and upload diagnostic reports with prescriptions - Access comprehensive patient medical history for informed decision-making **Usage Scenarios:** 1. Doctor reviews AI-suggested schedule for the day and makes adjustments 2. Doctor accesses patient health reports before a scheduled consultation 3. Doctor completes a consultation and submits diagnostic report with prescription #### 2.2.3 Family Member | Attribute | Description | |-----------|-------------| | **Role Code** | `FAMILY` | | **Description** | Relatives or caregivers of patients with limited, authorized access rights | | **Primary Permissions** | `FAMILY:READ` (limited scope) | **Key Needs:** - View authorized patient information as permitted by the patient - Receive emergency notifications about patient health status **Usage Scenarios:** 1. Family member checks on patient's recent health report status 2. Family member receives emergency notification about patient condition #### 2.2.4 System Administrator | Attribute | Description | |-----------|-------------| | **Role Code** | `ADMIN` | | **Description** | IT staff managing platform operations and configurations | | **Primary Permissions** | Full administrative access | **Key Needs:** - Manage user accounts and role assignments - Configure system permissions and access policies - Monitor system health and operational metrics --- ## 3. System Boundaries ### 3.1 System Scope Definition The ChronicCareAIPlatform is scoped as a **chronic disease care management platform** covering: - User authentication and authorization - Health report management and storage - Doctor scheduling and workbench functionality - Multi-channel messaging services ### 3.2 Included Core Components The following components are within the system boundary and are actively developed and maintained as part of this platform: ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "ChronicCareAIPlatform System Boundary" GW[API Gateway] AUTH[Auth Server] ACC[Account Management Service] HR[Health Report Center] DOC[Doctor Service] MSG[Message System] TP[Third-Party Service] UTIL[Utils Module] GW --> AUTH GW --> ACC GW --> HR GW --> DOC GW --> MSG AUTH --> ACC HR --> ACC HR --> TP DOC --> ACC MSG --> ACC MSG --> TP end ``` | Component | Responsibility | |-----------|----------------| | **API Gateway** | Request routing, JWT validation, rate limiting (Sentinel), CORS configuration, user context injection | | **Auth Server** | JWT token generation/validation, refresh token management, token blacklisting | | **Account Management Service** | User registration, login authentication, RBAC permissions, user identity management | | **Health Report Center** | Report upload, cloud storage integration, query/retrieval, audit workflow management | | **Doctor Service** | Schedule management, AI-powered recommendations, workbench APIs, consultation completion | | **Message System** | Push notifications, SMS delivery, email delivery, message templates | | **Third-Party Service** | OSS integration adapter, SMS provider adapter, email service adapter | | **Utils Module** | Security annotations, encryption handlers, interceptors, common utilities | ### 3.3 Excluded External Dependencies The following components and systems are **explicitly outside** the system boundary: | Category | Excluded Components | Rationale | |----------|---------------------|-----------| | **AI/ML Infrastructure** | AI/ML model training infrastructure | Platform consumes AI recommendations but does not train models | | **External Healthcare Systems** | Hospital Information Systems (HIS) | No direct integration with external hospital systems | | **Medical Devices** | Medical device integration | Out of scope for current platform version | | **Financial Systems** | Payment and billing systems | Healthcare billing handled externally | | **Real-time Communication** | Video consultation functionality | Not implemented in current scope | | **Client Applications** | Mobile application clients, Web frontend application | Backend-only scope; clients are separate projects | | **Analytics** | Data analytics and reporting dashboards | Business intelligence handled by separate systems | ### 3.4 System Boundary Diagram ```mermaid C4Context title System Context Diagram - ChronicCareAIPlatform Person(patient, "Patient", "Individual with chronic condition requiring ongoing care") Person(doctor, "Doctor", "Healthcare provider managing chronic disease patients") Person(family, "Family Member", "Caregiver with limited access rights") Person(admin, "System Administrator", "IT staff managing platform operations") System(platform, "ChronicCareAIPlatform", "Chronic disease care management platform providing health report management, doctor scheduling, and multi-channel messaging") System_Ext(oss, "Alibaba Cloud OSS", "Object storage for health report files") System_Ext(sms, "SMS Gateway", "Third-party SMS delivery service") System_Ext(email, "Email Service", "Email delivery service") System_Ext(nacos, "Nacos", "Service registry and configuration center") System_Ext(redis, "Redis", "Distributed cache for tokens and sessions") System_Ext(mysql, "MySQL", "Primary relational database") System_Ext(sentinel, "Sentinel", "Flow control and circuit breaker") Rel(patient, platform, "Uploads health reports, views records", "HTTPS/REST") Rel(doctor, platform, "Manages schedules, views patient reports", "HTTPS/REST") Rel(family, platform, "Views authorized patient info", "HTTPS/REST") Rel(admin, platform, "Manages accounts and permissions", "HTTPS/REST") Rel(platform, oss, "Stores/retrieves files", "REST API") Rel(platform, sms, "Sends SMS notifications", "REST API") Rel(platform, email, "Sends email notifications", "SMTP/REST") Rel(platform, nacos, "Service discovery", "Nacos Protocol") Rel(platform, redis, "Caches tokens/sessions", "Redis Protocol") Rel(platform, mysql, "Persists business data", "JDBC") Rel(platform, sentinel, "Rate limiting", "SDK") ``` --- ## 4. External System Interactions ### 4.1 External System Inventory The ChronicCareAIPlatform integrates with seven external systems to deliver its full functionality: | System | Category | Purpose | Criticality | |--------|----------|---------|-------------| | Alibaba Cloud OSS | Cloud Storage | Health report file storage | **High** | | SMS Gateway | Communication | Notification delivery | **Medium** | | Email Service | Communication | Notification delivery | **Medium** | | Nacos | Infrastructure | Service discovery & configuration | **Critical** | | Redis | Infrastructure | Token/session caching | **Critical** | | MySQL | Infrastructure | Primary data persistence | **Critical** | | Sentinel | Infrastructure | Rate limiting & circuit breaking | **High** | ### 4.2 Detailed Interaction Descriptions #### 4.2.1 Alibaba Cloud OSS ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant HC as Health Report Center participant TP as Third-Party Service participant OSS as Alibaba Cloud OSS HC->>TP: uploadFile(fileBytes, fileName) TP->>OSS: PUT /bucket/object (REST API) OSS-->>TP: File URL TP-->>HC: Permanent file URL Note over HC,OSS: Files stored with unique keys
URLs persisted in database ``` | Attribute | Details | |-----------|---------| | **Integration Type** | REST API via AliOSSUtils wrapper | | **Data Flow** | Outbound (file upload), Inbound (URL retrieval) | | **Authentication** | Alibaba Cloud AccessKey/SecretKey | | **Use Cases** | Health report images, PDF documents, diagnostic attachments | | **Failure Handling** | Retry with exponential backoff; fallback error response | #### 4.2.2 SMS Gateway | Attribute | Details | |-----------|---------| | **Integration Type** | REST API with rate limiting | | **Data Flow** | Outbound only | | **Rate Limiting** | Implemented via SMSRateLimiter to prevent abuse | | **Use Cases** | Verification codes, appointment reminders, emergency notifications | | **Failure Handling** | Queue for retry; log delivery failures | #### 4.2.3 Email Service | Attribute | Details | |-----------|---------| | **Integration Type** | SMTP/REST API | | **Data Flow** | Outbound only | | **Capabilities** | Plain text emails, emails with attachments | | **Use Cases** | Appointment confirmations, health report notifications, system alerts | | **Failure Handling** | Async delivery with retry mechanism | #### 4.2.4 Nacos (Service Registry) | Attribute | Details | |-----------|---------| | **Integration Type** | Service Discovery Protocol | | **Data Flow** | Bidirectional (registration/discovery) | | **Purpose** | Microservice registration, service discovery, configuration management | | **Criticality** | **Critical** - System cannot function without service discovery | | **Failure Handling** | Local cache of service instances; graceful degradation | #### 4.2.5 Redis | Attribute | Details | |-----------|---------| | **Integration Type** | Redis Protocol | | **Data Flow** | Bidirectional | | **Data Stored** | JWT refresh tokens, token blacklist, permission cache, session data | | **Criticality** | **Critical** - Authentication fails without Redis | | **Failure Handling** | Circuit breaker; fallback to database for critical operations | #### 4.2.6 MySQL | Attribute | Details | |-----------|---------| | **Integration Type** | JDBC via MyBatis-Plus ORM | | **Data Flow** | Bidirectional | | **Data Stored** | All business entities (accounts, reports, schedules, messages) | | **Criticality** | **Critical** - Primary data store | | **Features** | Sensitive data encryption via TypeHandler, optimistic locking | #### 4.2.7 Sentinel | Attribute | Details | |-----------|---------| | **Integration Type** | Sentinel SDK | | **Data Flow** | Internal monitoring and control | | **Purpose** | API rate limiting, circuit breaking, flow control | | **Configuration** | Per-route rate limits configured in SentinelGatewayConfig | | **Failure Handling** | Custom block response via SentinelGatewayBlockConfig | ### 4.3 Dependency Relationship Analysis ```mermaid graph LR subgraph "Critical Dependencies" NACOS[Nacos] REDIS[Redis] MYSQL[MySQL] end subgraph "High Priority Dependencies" OSS[Alibaba OSS] SENTINEL[Sentinel] end subgraph "Medium Priority Dependencies" SMS[SMS Gateway] EMAIL[Email Service] end PLATFORM[ChronicCareAIPlatform] PLATFORM -.->|"Service Discovery"| NACOS PLATFORM -.->|"Token/Session"| REDIS PLATFORM -.->|"Business Data"| MYSQL PLATFORM -->|"File Storage"| OSS PLATFORM -->|"Rate Limiting"| SENTINEL PLATFORM -->|"Notifications"| SMS PLATFORM -->|"Notifications"| EMAIL style NACOS fill:#ff6b6b style REDIS fill:#ff6b6b style MYSQL fill:#ff6b6b style OSS fill:#feca57 style SENTINEL fill:#feca57 style SMS fill:#48dbfb style EMAIL fill:#48dbfb ``` **Dependency Criticality Matrix:** | Dependency | Failure Impact | Recovery Strategy | |------------|----------------|-------------------| | Nacos | **System-wide outage** | Local service cache, manual failover | | Redis | **Authentication failure** | Database fallback for critical tokens | | MySQL | **Complete data loss** | Read replicas, backup restoration | | OSS | **File upload/download failure** | Queue uploads, retry mechanism | | Sentinel | **No rate limiting** | Graceful degradation, manual limits | | SMS/Email | **Notification delays** | Queue messages, retry later | --- ## 5. System Context Diagram ### 5.1 High-Level System Context ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Users" P[👤 Patient] D[👨‍⚕️ Doctor] F[👨‍👩‍👧 Family Member] A[🔧 System Admin] end subgraph "ChronicCareAIPlatform" GW[🚪 API Gateway] subgraph "Core Services" AUTH[🔐 Auth Server] ACC[👥 Account Management] HR[📋 Health Report Center] DOC[📅 Doctor Service] MSG[📨 Message System] end subgraph "Infrastructure Services" TP[🔌 Third-Party Service] UTIL[🛠️ Utils Module] end end subgraph "External Systems" OSS[(☁️ Alibaba OSS)] SMS[📱 SMS Gateway] EMAIL[📧 Email Service] NACOS[🔍 Nacos] REDIS[(💾 Redis)] MYSQL[(🗄️ MySQL)] SENT[⚡ Sentinel] end P & D & F & A -->|HTTPS/REST| GW GW --> AUTH & ACC & HR & DOC & MSG AUTH --> ACC HR --> ACC & TP DOC --> ACC MSG --> ACC & TP TP --> OSS & SMS & EMAIL GW --> SENT AUTH & ACC --> REDIS AUTH & ACC & HR & DOC & MSG --> MYSQL GW & AUTH & ACC & HR & DOC & MSG --> NACOS ``` ### 5.2 Key Interaction Flows #### 5.2.1 User Authentication Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Client participant Gateway as API Gateway participant Auth as Auth Server participant Account as Account Management participant Redis Client->>Gateway: POST /auth/login Gateway->>Auth: Route to auth-consumer Auth->>Account: Verify credentials (Dubbo RPC) Account->>Account: BCrypt password verification Account-->>Auth: User info + roles Auth->>Account: Get user permissions Account-->>Auth: Permission list Auth->>Auth: Generate JWT token pair Auth->>Redis: Store refresh token Auth-->>Gateway: Token pair + user info Gateway-->>Client: Login response ``` #### 5.2.2 Health Report Upload Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Patient participant Gateway as API Gateway participant HR as Health Report Center participant Account as Account Management participant OSS as Alibaba OSS Patient->>Gateway: POST /health/report/upload Gateway->>Gateway: Validate JWT token Gateway->>HR: Route with user context headers HR->>HR: Validate file type/size HR->>Account: Get patient info (attending doctor) Account-->>HR: Patient details HR->>OSS: Upload file bytes OSS-->>HR: File URL HR->>HR: Persist report record HR-->>Gateway: Upload success Gateway-->>Patient: Report ID + URL ``` #### 5.2.3 Doctor Schedule Management Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Doctor participant Gateway as API Gateway participant DS as Doctor Service participant Account as Account Management Doctor->>Gateway: GET /doctor/workbench/schedule/suggestion Gateway->>Gateway: Validate JWT + DOCTOR role Gateway->>DS: Route with user context DS->>DS: Generate AI schedule suggestions DS-->>Gateway: Suggested schedules Gateway-->>Doctor: Schedule recommendations Doctor->>Gateway: POST /doctor/workbench/schedule/complete Gateway->>DS: Complete schedule request DS->>DS: Validate state transition DS->>DS: Persist diagnostic report DS-->>Gateway: Completion confirmation Gateway-->>Doctor: Success response ``` ### 5.3 Architecture Decision Records | Decision | Rationale | Trade-offs | |----------|-----------|------------| | **Dual-Token Authentication** | Balances security (short-lived access tokens) with UX (long-lived refresh tokens) | Increased complexity; requires Redis for refresh token storage | | **Dubbo RPC for Inter-Service Communication** | High performance, type-safe contracts, native Spring Boot integration | Tighter coupling than REST; requires Nacos for service discovery | | **API Gateway Pattern** | Centralized security, rate limiting, and routing | Single point of failure; requires high availability setup | | **RBAC with Permission Expansion** | Flexible permission model supporting role-based and action-based access | Permission explosion risk; requires careful design | | **AES-CBC Encryption at Persistence Layer** | Transparent encryption of sensitive data without application code changes | Performance overhead; key management complexity | --- ## 6. Technical Architecture Overview ### 6.1 Main Technology Stack ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Presentation Layer" REST[REST APIs] JWT[JWT Authentication] end subgraph "Application Layer" SB[Spring Boot 3.x] DUBBO[Dubbo 3.x RPC] SS[Spring Security] end subgraph "Data Layer" MP[MyBatis-Plus ORM] REDIS_CLIENT[Redis Client] end subgraph "Infrastructure Layer" NACOS_CLIENT[Nacos Client] SENTINEL_CLIENT[Sentinel Client] OSS_SDK[Alibaba OSS SDK] end REST --> SB JWT --> SS SB --> DUBBO SB --> MP SB --> REDIS_CLIENT SB --> NACOS_CLIENT SB --> SENTINEL_CLIENT SB --> OSS_SDK ``` | Layer | Technology | Version | Purpose | |-------|------------|---------|---------| | **Runtime** | Spring Boot | 3.x | Application framework | | **RPC** | Apache Dubbo | 3.x | Inter-service communication | | **Security** | Spring Security + Nimbus JWT | Latest | Authentication & authorization | | **ORM** | MyBatis-Plus | Latest | Database access | | **Cache** | Redis | 6.x+ | Token storage, caching | | **Service Discovery** | Nacos | 2.x | Registry & configuration | | **Rate Limiting** | Alibaba Sentinel | Latest | Flow control | | **Cloud Storage** | Alibaba Cloud OSS | Latest | File storage | ### 6.2 Architecture Patterns #### 6.2.1 Three-Tier Module Pattern Each domain service follows a consistent three-tier structure: ``` domain-service/ ├── domain-api/ # Shared contracts (interfaces, DTOs, entities) ├── domain-consumer/ # BFF layer (REST controllers, security) └── domain-provider/ # Business logic (Dubbo services, data access) ``` **Benefits:** - Clear separation of concerns - Type-safe RPC contracts - Independent deployment of consumer and provider - Reusable API contracts across services #### 6.2.2 Security Architecture ```mermaid graph LR subgraph "Gateway Layer" GW[API Gateway] JWT_FILTER[JWT Auth Filter] SENTINEL_FILTER[Sentinel Filter] end subgraph "Service Layer" INTERCEPTOR[User Info Interceptor] AOP[Permission Check AOP] ANNOTATIONS["@RequireRole
@RequirePermission"] end subgraph "Data Layer" ENCRYPT[AES Encryption Handler] XSS[XSS/SQL Filter] end GW --> JWT_FILTER --> SENTINEL_FILTER JWT_FILTER --> INTERCEPTOR --> AOP --> ANNOTATIONS ANNOTATIONS --> ENCRYPT --> XSS ``` ### 6.3 Key Design Decisions | Decision Area | Approach | Justification | |---------------|----------|---------------| | **Authentication** | Stateless JWT with Redis-backed refresh tokens | Scalability without session affinity; secure token rotation | | **Authorization** | Declarative annotations with AOP enforcement | Clean code; centralized policy enforcement | | **Data Encryption** | Transparent MyBatis TypeHandler | No application code changes; consistent encryption | | **Service Communication** | Dubbo RPC over REST | Performance; type safety; native Spring integration | | **Rate Limiting** | Gateway-level Sentinel | Centralized protection; configurable per-route limits | | **File Storage** | Cloud OSS with URL persistence | Scalability; CDN integration; cost efficiency | --- ## 7. Summary The ChronicCareAIPlatform represents a well-architected microservices solution for chronic disease care management. Key architectural strengths include: 1. **Clear Domain Boundaries**: Eight distinct domain modules with well-defined responsibilities 2. **Robust Security Model**: Dual-token authentication with RBAC and declarative permission controls 3. **Scalable Infrastructure**: Cloud-native design with service discovery, distributed caching, and rate limiting 4. **Extensible Integration Layer**: Adapter pattern for third-party services enabling easy provider substitution The system successfully balances security, performance, and maintainability while providing a solid foundation for future enhancements such as AI model integration, mobile client support, and analytics capabilities. --- ## Appendix A: Glossary | Term | Definition | |------|------------| | **BFF** | Backend for Frontend - thin controller layer handling HTTP requests | | **Dubbo RPC** | High-performance RPC framework for microservice communication | | **JWT** | JSON Web Token - stateless authentication token format | | **RBAC** | Role-Based Access Control - permission model based on user roles | | **TypeHandler** | MyBatis component for custom type conversion (used for encryption) | | **Sentinel** | Alibaba flow control component for rate limiting and circuit breaking | ## Appendix B: Document Revision History | Version | Date | Author | Changes | |---------|------|--------|---------| | 1.0 | 2025-01-09 | Architecture Documentation System | Initial document generation |
